description: Error classification taxonomy for outbox sweep retry decisions. Each entry maps an error signal (substring or exception class) to a retry class and handling action.

RETRYABLE.description: Transient error. Same payload will succeed on retry after backoff.
RETRYABLE.action: Increment retry_count. Set next_retry_at with exponential backoff. Status = FAILED.

NON_RETRYABLE.description: Payload or schema error. Retrying will always fail. Escalate immediately to dead letter.
NON_RETRYABLE.action: Status = DEAD_LETTER. Write to dead_letter_events. No retry.

ALREADY_SYNCED.description: Sink already contains this event (idempotent replay). Not a failure.
ALREADY_SYNCED.action: Status = SYNCED. Write ledger entry with delivery_attempt > 1.

UNKNOWN.description: Error not matched by any rule. Treat as RETRYABLE with max 2 extra attempts before DLQ.
UNKNOWN.action: Treat as RETRYABLE but max_retries reduced to 2 for this class.

clickhouse.description: ClickHouse server unreachable — transient network or restart
Query timeout — server overloaded, retry after backoff
ClickHouse merge throttling — back off, let compaction complete
Server memory pressure — retry with smaller batch
Target table does not exist — schema mismatch, requires operator intervention
Payload type or format mismatch — payload is malformed, retry will not help
Credentials or permission error — requires operator fix before any events can be delivered
Row already present in ClickHouse — treat as successful idempotent delivery
Database does not exist — configuration error
Malformed query — generated SQL has syntax error, code bug
Mid-write connection drop — retry is safe (ClickHouse insert is transactional)

neo4j.description: Neo4j container unreachable — transient restart or Docker stop

error_signals: session has been marked as failed
neo4j.description: Session timeout — retry with new session
Transient server-side error — retry is explicitly recommended by Neo4j protocol
Node/relationship already exists with unique constraint — idempotent MERGE confirms delivery
Cypher query syntax error — code bug, not data issue
Auth rate limit — retry after backoff
Credentials invalid — operator must fix before events can be delivered
Schema element referenced does not exist in DB — schema mismatch
Malformed request — payload or query generator has a bug
Mid-write connection drop — MERGE is idempotent, retry is safe

lookup_algorithm.description: To classify an error, iterate rules for the relevant sink_target in order. For each rule, check if ANY error_signal string appears as a substring (case-insensitive) in the error message. Return the class of the first matching rule. If no rule matches, return UNKNOWN.

dead_letter_escalation_policy.RETRYABLE_EXHAUSTED: retry_count >= max_retries on RETRYABLE error → DEAD_LETTER
dead_letter_escalation_policy.NON_RETRYABLE: Any NON_RETRYABLE error on any attempt → immediate DEAD_LETTER
